programmes.show_description: Nine Lives: James Bolam and Amanda Redman star in the popular crime drama. An elderly lady's body is discovered, partly consumed by her horde of cats. Jack thinks it's murder.
Dr Turner helps a couple whose daughter was born without limbs to have her fitted for prosthetics - but the experience provokes different reactions from her mother and father.
Sister Julienne oversees the opening of a family planning clinic at the local community centre, leading a mother of three to start taking the contraceptive pill behind her husband's back.
Noble Rot: After wine critic and connoisseur Raymond Bettane collapses and dies during a tasting, Mick and Shazia find a broken champagne flute and a crushed glass vial.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Breadwinner: Essex humour with the Chigwell sisters. Sharon's cafe is doing well, but Tracey finds her success hard to take. Dorien is in pursuit of a new relationship.
When the Snow Lay Round About: It's two nights before Christmas, and the whole of Sun Hill is desperately hoping there won't be a shout. Pretty unlikely!
New. Life's a Beach: Archie deals with a case of an arrogant medical student's food poisoning, while Jimmi worries that things are moving too quickly with Eva.
New. It's a Sin: Michelle makes Ruth wear a flattering top, but the receptionist knows all too well the consequences if Davey sees her.
Conflict of Interests: Connie and Zubin clash over the treatment of a patient, Donna is uncomfortable about her night with Mickie, and Diane tries to put her life back in order.
What Lies Beneath: Jeff retaliates against unscrupulous gang leader Karl Fontayne. Mads is torn between the values of her old and new lives. Lenny tries not to give his sister bad news.
When the Snow Lay Round About: It's two nights before Christmas, and the whole of Sun Hill is desperately hoping there won't be a shout. Pretty unlikely!
New. Will Janine be able to admit her true feelings before it is too late? Danny's mask begins to slip, and Ronnie struggles through a difficult day.
New. Billie makes a decision that could change his life forever. Janine, meanwhile, attempts to do the right thing, and Ben's worst fears become sickening reality.
The Female of the Species: Robert Hardy stars in the veterinary drama ideal with a cup of tea and a biscuit. Calum becomes more enthusiastic when he meets the new veterinary student.
James reveals the truth about his tragic past to Jan, while Laura and Ken have a disagreement over the commissioning of the trailer-sailer.
Hunger for Bread: Lu infiltrates a local slimming club to discover their secrets, but gets more than she bargained for when she realises her sister is a member.
Thirty Something: Sibling sitcom and the Essex girls have the birthday blues. Sharon is approaching her 30th birthday but is unable to see a bright future ahead of her.
How Not to Cry at Weddings: The world's longest-running sitcom here draws to a poignant end. Everyone is getting ready for the wedding. Clegg wonders if his suit smells of mothballs.
Of Funerals and Fish: The very first episode of the longest running sitcom in the world! When two men remove Compo's television, he retaliates by stealing Nora Batty's bloomers.
Short Back and Palais Glide: It's Blamire, not Foggy, who appears here in the first series of the long-running sitcom. The boys discuss life, love and unpleasant women.
Blood and Water: Detective Inspector Mike Shepherd is sent to the New Zealand town of Brokenwood to investigate the death of a farmer in this opening episode of the drama series.
Nine Lives: James Bolam and Amanda Redman star in the popular crime drama. An elderly lady's body is discovered, partly consumed by her horde of cats. Jack thinks it's murder.
In the Guise of Death: Compelling crime-cracking with the inspector and his down-to-earth lady chum. A horse trainer is found hanged in his stable, but Lynley doubts it was suicide.
The Female of the Species: Robert Hardy stars in the veterinary drama ideal with a cup of tea and a biscuit. Calum becomes more enthusiastic when he meets the new veterinary student.
Death n' Bass: Following Brokenwood's first drum 'n' bass festival, the van belonging to the band Starscraper suddenly explodes.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Food for Thought: Chigwell-set comedy featuring the co-habiting sisters and their oversexed neighbour. Garth causes a stir when he says he doesn't want to work in the city.
Blowing It All Away: PC Rickman becomes increasingly friendly with a drug user and soon comes under suspicion of using drugs herself.
New. Need to Know: An ex-girlfriend of Nick's turns up at the Mill, eager to turn the clock back. Vivien has a disastrous driving lesson when Eva comes along for the ride.
New. Walking Wounded: Daniel and Archie are obliged to work together on the case of a man forced to revisit his troubled past, and Davey flirts with Michelle in front of Ruth.
Inside Out: Diane brings Jack to work with her and is warned that she must choose between motherhood and her career. Mubbs tries to make amends with Rosie.
Season of Goodwill: Kirsty makes an important decision about her future after Warren is admitted to the ED. Yuki helps Lenny in his desperate search to find a donor match for his sister.
Blowing It All Away: PC Rickman becomes increasingly friendly with a drug user and soon comes under suspicion of using drugs herself.
New. Billie is determined to serve his country, but furious Carol has other ideas. Syed and Amira's windfall moves them away from Zainab, and Leon shows Zsa Zsa his romantic side.
New. Billie will not be budged on his plans to fight for Britain. Meanwhile, Amira unwittingly torments Zainab, and Danny plays Ronnie and Roxy off against each other.
The Jackpot: Vintage stuff from TV's golden age, with Christopher Timothy as the resourceful vet. James is challenged to a race, and a retired sheepdog takes up chasing cars.
Jan has a confrontation with Ken over his plans to buy into her fashion business. Meanwhile, Charles and Gerald prepare for their trial.
Die We Must: A missing manuscript case takes a dramatic turn when Frank and Lu find themselves trapped in a famous author's house, where no one is what they seem.
Love on the Run: More Essex comedy from the Chigwell sisters. Darryl has been taken to hospital with appendicitis and plans to escape and spend the night with his wife.
Inventor of the 40-foot Ferret: The sitcom's first series, with the loveable fellows enjoying a second childhood in Yorkshire. Clegg and Blamire encourage Compo's spirituality.
Pate and Chips: Classic comic adventures from the early days when Michael Bates was one of the naughty silvered trio. The chums go to a stately home, with predictable consequences!
Spring Fever: Compo makes an effort by dressing up smartly as he attempts to woo a lady. So he might take the ferrets out of his trousers... fun in the early days of the sitcom.

programmes.show_name: PD James: Death in Holy Orders
programmes.show_description: Atmospheric feature-length murder-mystery thriller, based on the PD James book. Adam Dalgliesh investigates a death and cover-up at a theological college he knows well.
Buried Treasure: Alun Armstrong and James Bolam star in the hugely popular crime caper. When Brian's dog finds a body on a common, it prompts two people to confess to murder.
